name: Densify Cloud Resource Optimization API
what_it_does: ML-powered cloud resource optimization platform with REST API — analyzing workload patterns to recommend precise instance rightsizing, container resource (CPU/memory) right-sizing for Kubernetes, cloud cost optimization across AWS/Azure/GCP, and continuously updating recommendations as workloads change.
use_cases: Querying ML-generated instance rightsizing recommendations for engineering cost review
Pulling Kubernetes container resource recommendations for DevOps pipeline integration
Integrating Densify optimization actions with infrastructure automation workflows
Monitoring realized savings from applied recommendations for FinOps reporting
Automating resource size policy enforcement via Densify recommendation API
not_for: Multi-cloud FinOps reporting without optimization focus (use Vantage or CloudCheckr)
Spot instance management and interruption handling (use nOps or CAST AI)
Application performance monitoring without resource management (use Datadog)
best_when: An engineering or FinOps team wants ML-driven, workload-pattern-specific resource rightsizing recommendations for cloud instances and Kubernetes containers, with API integration into infrastructure automation pipelines.
avoid_when: You need simple cost visibility reports, Spot instance automation, or application performance monitoring beyond resource optimization.

auth.notes: Bearer token auth from Densify account. API token scoped to organization. No webhooks. Cloud provider IAM roles required for workload analysis (AWS IAM, Azure AD, GCP service account). Kubernetes agent deployment required for container optimization.

pricing.paid_tiers: Per-resource subscription; contact sales for pricing
pricing.notes: Canadian company (Toronto/Mississauga). Founded 2010. Privately held. ML optimization differentiator — recommendations based on actual workload behavior, not generic rules. Kubernetes-native rightsizing for containers and pods. Multi-cloud: AWS, Azure, GCP, on-prem VMware. API-first for GitOps and infrastructure automation. Strong in enterprise DevOps and FinOps teams.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: REST API for recommendations, workloads, and realized savings. Bearer token auth. Good API documentation for enterprise integration. Cloud provider IAM roles required for workload data collection. Kubernetes agent deployment required for container optimization. Recommendations updated as workloads change — implement refresh cadence.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Recommendation reads fully idempotent. Workload reads idempotent. Savings reads idempotent by period.
known_agent_gotchas: Cloud provider IAM roles required for all clouds — least privilege scoping needed
Kubernetes agent deployment required for container optimization
ML recommendations need 2-4 weeks of workload data before first recommendations
Resource changes based on recommendations are actual infrastructure ops — implement change management
Annual contract — validate ROI before infrastructure automation pipeline investment

security_score_components.security_notes: TLS enforced. SOC2 and ISO27001 compliant. US and Canadian data residency. Bearer token auth. Cloud provider IAM roles are privileged — implement least privilege for cost and workload data read access. Kubernetes agent has pod metrics access — review RBAC. Resource optimization actions are production changes — implement approval workflow.
